content:
Identifying Strengths and Weaknesses: Reflecting on your essay allows you to determine what you did well and where you struggled. Did you effectively communicate your ideas? Did your arguments flow logically? Were there areas of confusion?

Gaining Self-awareness: Self-awareness is key to improvement. Reflecting on your work helps you understand your writing habits, preferences, and areas that need development. This self-awareness is invaluable for future writing endeavors.

Description, Feelings, Evaluation, Analysis, Conclusion, and Action Plan

Long Description

Reference
Gibbs, G. (1988). Learning by doing: A guide to teaching & Learning methods. Oxford Brooks University
After you finish this page, click Next to continue to Steps for Effective Reflection.

Week 6 | Steps for Effective Reflection

Steps for Effective Reflection
To make the most of your reflection process, follow these steps:

Read Your Essay Critically: Start by reading your essay as if you were a discerning reader. Note any areas where the text could be clearer, more concise, or more engaging.
